learning_objectives: Understand California's multi-layer regulatory framework (federal, state, local)
Navigate Colorado's radiation control program requirements
Compare and contrast permit requirements between states
Identify when local fire marshal approval is required
Apply scenario-based decision making for interstate operations

phases.title: Scenario Warm-Up: The West Coast Tour
phases.description: You're planning a laser show tour hitting venues in Los Angeles, San Diego, and Denver. What permits will you need?

interactive_element.prompt: Select each location to discover permit requirements:

feedback.correct_understanding: Correct! California requires federal, state, AND local permits, while Colorado primarily defers to federal requirements with state notification.
feedback.key_insight: California has one of the most complex multi-layer regulatory systems in the US.

content_sections.title: Federal Layer (FDA)
content_sections.content: All laser shows in California require FDA variance under 21 CFR 1040.11. This is the baseline federal requirement.
key_points: Variance Application (Form available on FDA website)

content_sections.title: State Layer (CDPH)
content_sections.content: California Department of Public Health requires registration of Class IIIb and IV lasers.

content_sections.title: Local Layer (City/County Fire)
content_sections.content: Based on San Diego guidelines, local fire departments require permits for special events with lasers.
key_points: Site plans and floor plans required
Fee based on current fee schedule

phases.title: State Comparator: California vs Colorado
phases.description: Interactive side-by-side comparison of state requirements

categories.name: Regulatory Authority
categories.note: CA has multiple agencies; CO consolidated under radiation program

categories.name: Device Registration
categories.note: CA has formal registration; CO follows federal guidance

categories.name: Entertainment Shows
categories.note: CA requires local approval; CO defers to federal

phases.title: Permit Requirement Finder
phases.description: Determine exactly what permits you need based on your situation

questions.question: Which state will you operate in?
What type of venue in California?
What type of venue in Colorado?

r_ca_outdoor.title: California Outdoor Public Event Permits
permits: Local Fire Special Event Permit (2+ weeks advance)

r_ca_private.title: California Private Studio
r_ca_private.note: No local permits if truly private with no employees

r_ca_small.title: California Small Indoor Event
r_ca_small.note: May not need local fire permit under 50 people

phases.title: Licensing Timeline Visualizer
phases.description: Visual timeline showing the permit process for each state

events.action: San Diego: sdfdevents@sandiego.gov with site plans
Await approval, respond to any requests
Work with venue on safety requirements

phases.title: Challenge: Multi-State Scenario
phases.description: Apply your knowledge to a complex interstate operation

interactive_element.title: The Multi-State Festival Circuit
interactive_element.scenario: You're the LSO for a touring EDM festival hitting Sacramento (CA), then moving to Denver (CO) the following weekend. The festival features Class 4 lasers with audience scanning. Today is March 1st. The first show is March 25th.

questions.question: What's your immediate priority for California?
options: Contact Sacramento Fire Department for special event permit
questions.explanation: You need BOTH federal variance AND local fire permit. With only 3+ weeks, you need to start both processes immediately. The FDA variance can take 2-4 weeks, and CA local permits require 2+ weeks.

questions.question: What additional requirement does Sacramento likely have?
questions.explanation: California requires state CDPH registration AND if it's an outdoor show, FAA notification may be required depending on proximity to airspace.

questions.question: For the Denver show, what permits are needed beyond FDA variance?
questions.explanation: Colorado primarily requires notifying the Radiation Control Program. Unlike California, there's no separate state licensing requirement, though Denver may have local venue requirements.

badge.name: Multi-State Navigator
badge.description: Successfully navigated complex interstate regulatory requirements

quiz_questions.question: Which agency regulates laser devices at the state level in California?
quiz_questions.explanation: California Department of Public Health (CDPH) regulates laser devices at the state level.

quiz_questions.question: How far in advance must San Diego special event permits be submitted?
quiz_questions.explanation: San Diego Fire-Rescue requires special event permit applications to be submitted at least two weeks prior to the event.

quiz_questions.question: Colorado requires state-level laser device registration for all Class 4 lasers.
quiz_questions.explanation: Colorado defers to federal regulations and does not require separate state registration. The Radiation Control Program should be notified, but there's no formal registration requirement.

quiz_questions.question: You're planning a laser show in Los Angeles with 200 attendees. What permits are required?
options: FDA variance + CA CDPH registration + LA Fire permit
FDA variance + LA Fire permit only
quiz_questions.explanation: California requires all three: FDA variance (federal), CDPH registration (state), AND local fire permit for events with 50+ people.

quiz_questions.question: A touring show has an FDA variance and CA CDPH registration. They want to add a last-minute Denver show. What must they do?
quiz_questions.correct_answer: Notify Colorado Radiation Control Program and ensure venue compliance with federal variance terms
quiz_questions.explanation: Colorado requires notification to the Radiation Control Program but doesn't require separate state licensing if federal variance is in place.

key_takeaways: California has a three-tier system: Federal (FDA) + State (CDPH) + Local (Fire)
Colorado primarily defers to federal requirements with state notification
San Diego requires 2+ weeks advance notice for special event permits
California events with 50+ people require local fire permits
Both states require FDA variance for entertainment laser shows
Planning timeline: California = 3-4 weeks minimum, Colorado = 2-3 weeks
